11 May 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • US, Iran Clash Over Peace Proposal As Gulf Stalemate Continues RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump described the cease-fire with Iran as being on "life support," adding he is considering restarting naval escorts -- dubbed Operation Enduring Freedom -- through the Strait of Hormuz amid a continuing a deadlock over how to bring the conflict to an end.

  • South Korea Condemns Attack On Cargo Ship In Strait Of Hormuz, Vows Response RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- Seoul has strongly condemned the attack on a cargo ship belonging to a Korean shipping company in the Strait of Hormuz earlier this month, saying it would respond after identifying the perpetrator of the attack.
  • Could Iran Start Charging Global Tech Firms For Undersea Cables In The Strait Of Hormuz? RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- Iran has insisted for weeks that it has the right to charge international ships to transit the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping route off the coast of the Islamic republic.
  • Iran Says It Allowed Iraqi Tanker To Pass Through Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- A VLCC tanker named Agus Fanorius passed through the route designated by Iran in the Strait of Hormuz, according the semiofficial Tasnim news agency, which is linked to Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps.

  • Trump Says Iran Cease-Fire On 'Life Support' RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump characterized the cease-fire with Iran as being on "life support," adding he is considering restarting naval escorts -- dubbed Operation Enduring Freedom -- through the Strait of Hormuz as he looks to achieve "complete victory" in the war.
  • Trump Says Diplomatic Resolution of Conflict With Iran is Still 'Very Possible' Sputnik 11 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Monday that a diplomatic resolution to the conflict with Iran is still "very possible."
  • Trump Says Will 'Deal With' Current Iranian Leadership Until 'They Make Deal' Sputnik 11 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Monday that he will "deal with" the current Iranian leadership until the peace deal is reached.
  • Trump Calls Iran's Response to US Proposal to End Conflict Unacceptable Sputnik 11 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump on Sunday called Iran's response to the US proposal to end the conflict completely unacceptable.
  • New Round of Economic Fury Sanctions Targets IRGC Oil Operations US Dept. of State 11 May 2026 -- The Trump Administration is intensifying pressure on the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) by targeting the financial networks that enable its illicit oil operations.
  • Economic Fury Ramps Up Pressure on Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Oil Operations US Treasury 11 May 2026 -- Today, the Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) is designating 12 individuals and entities for their roles enabling the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' (IRGC) sale and shipment of Iranian oil to the People's Republic of China.
  • Trump Rejects Iran's Response To Peace Proposal As 'Totally Unacceptable' RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump angrily rejected Tehran's response to a US peace proposal as "totally unacceptable," while Iranian state media listed what appeared to be no easing of the Islamic republic's demands, including compensation for war damages and control over the Strait of Hormuz.

  • Iran rejects U.S. ceasefire proposal, Trump deems it "unacceptable" People's Daily 11 May 2026 -- Iran rejected the latest U.S. proposal as tantamount to surrender, calling instead for an end to the war, the removal of sanctions and the lifting of the naval blockade in its response to the draft plan, which U.S. President Donald Trump described as "totally unacceptable."
  • Iran's armed forces ready to shock aggressors: Speaker Qalibaf Press TV 11 May 2026 -- Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf has stated that the Islamic Republic's armed forces are battle-ready to deliver a crushing, well-deserved response to any aggression, warning enemies that mistaken strategies will only bring them disastrous results.
  • Iran will defeat Trump in diplomacy as it did on battlefield: Leader's advisor Press TV 11 May 2026 -- A top advisor to the Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei, says the US will never be able to win in diplomacy against Iran after it lost on the battlefield.
  • Iran says nuclear technology, enrichment not on agenda of talks with US Press TV 11 May 2026 -- Head of the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI) Mohammad Eslami told lawmakers the issues of nuclear technology and uranium enrichment are not on the agenda of any possible negotiations with the United States, as Tehran insists the talks must focus only on ending the war in the region.
  • Iran says its proposal was generous as US insists on 'unreasonable demands' Press TV 11 May 2026 -- Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei says Iran's proposal for ending the recent war was reasonable, as the United States continues to insist on "unreasonable demands" shaped by the Israeli regime.
  • Iran Ready to Discuss Nuclear Issue With US When Time Comes - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 11 May 2026 -- Iran does not rule out holding talks with the United States on the nuclear issue in the future and is ready to discuss it when the time comes, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei said on Monday.
  • Iran Is Not Criminal, But Fighter Against Criminals - Iranian Foreign Ministry Sputnik 11 May 2026 -- US is the biggest threat to peace in the world, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokeman Esmail Baghaei said on Monday.
  • Iran's draft proposal for U.S. talks demands ending war, removing sanctions, lifting naval blockade People's Daily 11 May 2026 -- Iran's latest draft proposal for talks with the United States calls for an immediate cessation of conflict on all fronts, a guarantee for no more "aggression" against Iran, and the lifting of U.S. sanctions and naval blockade, the semi-official Tasnim news agency reported Sunday.

  • Strait of Hormuz de-escalation is urgent, says UN chief UN News 11 May 2026 -- As the Strait of Hormuz crisis deepens and tensions between Iran and the United States remain unresolved, oil prices rose again early Monday, prompting the UN Secretary-General to call for a peaceful resolution and warn of the widening fallout across Africa and beyond.

  • Beijing Pushes Back On US Sanctions Tied To Iran War Ahead Of Trump-Xi Summit RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- Beijing says it opposes the inclusion of Chinese and Hong Kong entities in the latest round of US sanctions aimed at companies with ties to the Iranian regime, as US President Donald Trump prepares to head to China and meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping for the first time since 2017.

  • UK and France set to host multinational Strait of Hormuz meeting as British Warship to pre-position in the region UK MOD 11 May 2026 -- UK and France to host first meeting of Defence Ministers to advance the Strait of Hormuz multinational mission. This comes as HMS Dragon, one of the UK's most capable warships, will forward deploy to the region.
  • UK sanctions Iranian targets in response to national security threats UK FCDO 11 May 2026 -- Foreign Secretary announces sanctions against organisations and individuals enabling hostile Iranian activity
  • The State Defense Council approved Lithuania's participation in international maritime security operations in the Strait of Hormuz President of the Republic of Lithuania 11 May 2026 -- The State Defense Council, chaired by President Gitanas Nausėda, reviewed information and proposals submitted by the Ministry of National Defense of the Republic of Lithuania regarding Lithuania's contribution to ensuring freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. The Council decided to support Lithuania's participation in international efforts aimed at safeguarding freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Macron says 'never considered' French military deployment in Hormuz without 'coordination with Iran' Press TV 11 May 2026 -- French President Emmanuel Macron has ruled out any unilateral French military deployment in the Strait of Hormuz amid the US-Israeli war on Iran.
  • Yemen's Ansarullah warns US after Trump rejected Iran's peace plan Press TV 11 May 2026 -- A senior media official from Yemen's Ansarullah movement has warned that the United States must bear the consequences of rejecting Iran's latest proposal to end the war, following negative remarks by US President Donald Trump.

  • Former Senior US Diplomat Gordon Gray: Iran Talks Likely To Continue Despite Trump's Latest Rejection RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- Former senior US diplomat Gordon Gray, who served as deputy assistant secretary of state for Near Eastern affairs under President George W. Bush, says indirect negotiations between Washington and Tehran are likely to continue despite what he described as significant pain and pressure on both sides.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • FORGING THE DIGITAL BATTLESPACE: Five Eyes Allies Accelerate 'Project Arcadia' at the Combined Digital Leadership Summit DoD 11 May 2026 -- The summit's mandate aligns directly with the strategic imperatives set by Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, who has continually emphasized that technological and operational unity with our partners is the bedrock of global stability.
  • Department of the Navy Releases Fiscal Year 2027 Shipbuilding Plan US Navy Press Release 11 May 2026 -- The Department of the Navy today released its Fiscal Year 2027 Shipbuilding Plan, outlining the Administration's long-term strategy to expand fleet capacity, strengthen the maritime industrial base, and deliver a more capable, ready, and lethal naval force.
  • Department of the Navy Launches PAE Mission Systems to Accelerate Warfighting Capability at Speed US Navy 11 May 2026 -- The Department of the Navy today announced the establishment of the Portfolio Acquisition Executive (PAE) for Mission Systems, a new warfighting organization designed to accelerate the development and delivery of critical capabilities to the fleet.
  • Navy Establishes Portfolio Acquisition Executive for Munitions: Postured to harness innovation, scale production, and deliver capability and capacity sooner. US Navy 11 May 2026 -- The Department of the Navy announced May 11 the establishment of Portfolio Acquisition Executive (PAE) Munitions, which will provide focused, enterprise-level acquisition strategies, prioritization, and execution to urgently accelerate the delivery of munitions to the warfighter.
  • US Air Force reassigns combat training units to enhance readiness US Air Force 11 May 2026 -- The Department of Air Force has initiated the reassignment of combat air forces formal training units from Air Education and Training Command to Air Combat Command.
  • Historic African Lion 26 concludes AFRICOM 11 May 2026 -- After weeks of synchronized operations across land, air, sea, cyber, and space domains, African Lion 26 concluded with a series of complex, technology-driven culminating events that underscored the exercise's evolution into the premier joint, all-domain multinational training event on the continent, May 8, 2026.
  • US munitions 'shockingly' depleted by Iran war, US Senator warns Press TV 11 May 2026 -- The United States has drawn down its munitions stockpiles so deeply during the war with Iran that it could be left vulnerable to other conflicts, Democratic Senator Mark Kelly said on Sunday, describing the situation as "shocking."
  • Connecticut National Guard hosts Cyber Yankee 2026 Army News 11 May 2026 -- The Connecticut National Guard is hosting Cyber Yankee 2026, the National Guard's premier annual regional cyber training exercise, at Camp Nett in Connecticut, May 4-15.

  • Readout of Secretary of War Pete Hegseth's Meeting with Republic of Korea Minister of National Defense Ahn Gyu-back DoD 11 May 2026
  • Secretary of War Pete Hegseth Hosted Bilateral Meeting With the Republic of Korea Minister of National Defense Ahn Gyu-back at the Pentagon DoD 11 May 2026 -- SECRETARY OF WAR PETE HEGSETH: Well, Minister Ahn, it is an honor to welcome you and your team to here in the Pentagon. Thank you for making the trip to Washington. Our meeting today arrives at a pivotal moment for the US ROK Alliance. Together, our nations are taking strong actions to ensure our combined readiness and to defend vital national security interests.
  • Hegseth Welcomes South Korean Counterpart to Pentagon DoD News 11 May 2026 -- Secretary of War Pete Hegseth welcomed South Korean Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back to Washington today for a bilateral engagement at the Pentagon.

  • NATO's Special Representative for the Caucasus and Central Asia visits Georgia NATO 11 May 2026 -- On 7-8 May 2026, Ambassador Kevin Hamilton, the NATO Secretary General's Special Representative for the Caucasus and Central Asia, paid a visit to Georgia. He met with Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs Maka Botchorishvili, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defence Irakli Chikovani, and Deputy Foreign Minister Lasha Darsalia.

  • Development of Joint Defense Production, Strengthening Air Defense, and the Diplomatic Process: President Met with German Defense Minister President of Ukraine 11 May 2026
  • We Are Doing Our Best So That We Can Produce Enough Anti-Ballistic Missiles Here in Ukraine or Jointly with Partners - Address by the President President of Ukraine 11 May 2026
  • Andrii Sybiha held a meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 11 May 2026
  • Ukraine and Germany launch Brave Germany, a joint programme for defence technology development Ukraine MOD 11 May 2026 -- In Kyiv, Minister of Defence of Ukraine Mykhailo Fedorov and Federal Minister of Defence of Germany Boris Pistorius signed a letter of intent to launch Brave Germany, a joint initiative aimed at developing defence technologies and supporting innovative start-ups.
  • russia modified Kh-101 missiles four times to strengthen its aerial terror campaign — Ukraine still intercepts 88% Ukraine MOD 11 May 2026 -- Since the beginning of 2026, Ukraine's air defense has intercepted about 88% of the Kh-101, Kh-55, and Kh-555 cruise missiles launched by russia against Ukraine. Ukraine's air defense continues to demonstrate high effectiveness despite the enemy's attempts to improve its strike capabilities.
  • From training to AI: Ukraine and NATO outline key areas of cooperation within JATEC Ukraine MOD 11 May 2026 -- The current combat experience of the Defence Forces of Ukraine offers NATO countries a unique opportunity to launch new joint projects and strengthen existing ones, particularly through JATEC.

  • Minister Anand meets with NATO Secretary General in Brussels Canada 11 May 2026

  • DPP's colossal arms purchase bill suffers setback amid rising doubts People's Daily 11 May 2026 -- Taiwan's legislature has passed a revised version of a special arms procurement bill, cutting the total budget from the massive 1.25 trillion New Taiwan dollars (about 40 billion U.S. dollars) proposed by the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) authorities to 780 billion New Taiwan dollars.

  • NZ Army takes jungle warfare lessons from Philippines exercise New Zealand Defence Force 11 May 2026 -- The New Zealand Army has completed its first Exercise Balikatan in the Philippines, improving its ability to fight a modern war amid the heat, humidity and venomous snakes and insects of the jungle.

  • 2nd India-Japan Economic Security Dialogue India MEA 11 May 2026 -- Foreign Secretary Shri Vikram Misri co-chaired the 2nd round of the India-Japan Economic Security Dialogue in New Delhi on 11 May 2026 along with Vice Minister, Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan Mr. Takehiro Funakoshi and Vice Minister- International Affairs, Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry Mr. Takehiko Matsuo.
  • Indian Coast Guard Conducts Girder Laying Ceremony for Indigenous Air Cushion Vehicles in Goa India PIB 11 May 2026 -- The Girder Laying Ceremony of the 4th, 5th and 6th Air Cushion Vehicles (ACVs) for the Indian Coast Guard (ICG) was held at Rassaim Yard of Chowgule Shipyard, Goa, on May 11, 2026. Under the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative, these ACVs are being manufactured with 50% indigenous material in India, marking a significant leap in the collective pursuit of self-reliance and technological advancement.
  • Key takeaways of 5th IGoM on West Asia chaired by RM: No shortage of any petroleum product, India has 60 days of crude oil, 60 days of Natural Gas & 45 days of LPG rolling stock India PIB 11 May 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh chaired the 5th meeting of the Informal Group of Ministers (IGoM) on West Asia at Kartavya Bhawan-2, New Delhi on May 11, 2026.

  • Israel's Ben Gurion Airport turning into 'US military base,' official says Press TV 11 May 2026 -- The chief of Israel's civil aviation has warned that Ben Gurion Airport in Tel Aviv has effectively turned into a "US military base," disrupting civilian flights and threatening Israeli airlines.
  • 'We need more troops immediately': Israeli military chief warns amid multi-front war Press TV 11 May 2026 -- The Israeli regime's military chief has warned lawmakers that the military urgently needs more forces as it continues to wage war on multiple fronts, according to Hebrew media reports.
  • Netanyahu vows to phase out US aid, blames social media for turning Americans against Israel Press TV 11 May 2026 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has claimed that he plans to bring American financial aid to the Tel Aviv regime down to zero within the next decade, blaming social media platforms for "manipulating" the American public opinion towards Israel.

  • Estonia will purchase three additional Chunmoo multiple rocket launchers produced in South Korea Estonia MoD 11 May 2026 -- The Estonian Centre for Defence Investments (ECDI) has signed an additional contract with South Korea's procurement organisation KOTRA to procure three Chunmoo multiple rocket launchers from the defence company Hanwha Aerospace.

  • Baiba Braže discusses strengthening Latvia's air defence and NATO's eastern flank with the NATO Secretary General Latvia MFA 11 May 2026 -- On 11 May 2026, in Brussels, Belgium, the Minister of Foreign Affairs Baiba Braže met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte to discuss strengthening NATO's deterrence and defence capabilities, the security situation in the region, and preparations for the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara.

  • Minister of National Defence to attend the Foreign Affairs Council (Defence) in Brussels Lithuania MND 11 May 2026 -- On May 12 Minister of National Defence Robertas Kaunas will take part in the Foreign Affairs Council (Defence) in Brussels.

  • The Chairman of the NATO Military Committee concluded his official visit to Romania Romania - Ministry of National Defence 11 May 2026 -- On Friday, May 8th, The Chief of Defence Staff, General Gheorghiță Vlad, and the Chairman of the NATO Military Committee, Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, visited the Multinational Corps South-East Headquarters (MNC-SE HQ) in Sibiu and the "Getica" National Joint Training Centre in Cincu.

  • Azerbaijan, Slovakia explore military cooperation Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 May 2026 -- Azerbaijan's Minister of Defense, Colonel General Zakir Hasanov paid an official visit to the Republic of Slovakia at the invitation of Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense of Slovakia Robert Kaliňák, the Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • Navy improving anti-submarine warfare capabilities Philippine News Agency 11 May 2026 -- The Philippine Navy (PN) on Monday said it is committed to improving its "anti-submarine warfare" (ASW) capabilities as part of its efforts to safeguard the country's "sea lines of communications."
  • Canada, NZ defense pacts seen to boost PH supply chain security Philippine News Agency 11 May 2026 -- The proposed Visiting Forces Agreements (VFA) with Canada and New Zealand could help strengthen the Philippines' supply chain security and economic resilience by supporting freedom of navigation and regional stability, Defense Secretary Gilbert Teodoro Jr. told senators on Monday.

  • Trump 'frustrated' with Cuba's ability to withstand pressure, considers military action: Report Press TV 11 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has grown "increasingly frustrated" with Cuba and its ability to withstand months of US pressure, and is considering waging an act of aggression against the Caribbean country, according to a report.

  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Ethiopian Foreign Minister Gedion US Dept. of State 11 May 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Australian Minister for Foreign Affairs Wong US Dept. of State 11 May 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with UK Foreign Secretary Cooper US Dept. of State 11 May 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 11 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 11 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Lebanon Appeals To US To Help Stop Israeli Attacks RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- Lebanese President Joseph Aoun said on May 11 that he had asked the United States to pressure Israel to respect the cease-fire and halt attacks on civilian homes in southern Lebanon.

  • Hezbollah strikes crushing blows against Israeli troops in southern Lebanon Press TV 11 May 2026 -- The Islamic resistance movement Hezbollah has carried out 20 defensive military operations against invading Israeli occupation forces in southern Lebanon, delivering heavy losses to the aggressors and proving once again the determination to defend Lebanese sovereignty.
  • New Israeli attacks kill six civilians across southern Lebanon amid truce violations Press TV 11 May 2026 -- At least six people have been killed and several others wounded as Israeli occupying forces carried out a series of attacks targeting multiple areas in southern Lebanon.
  • Lebanon's president to US envoy: Pressure Israel to end ceasefire violations Press TV 11 May 2026 -- Lebanese President Joseph Aoun has called on the United States to pressure Israel into halting its recurrent violations of a ceasefire agreement with the Beirut government-- daily strikes against towns and villages in southern Lebanon and demolitions of residential buildings.
  • One Israeli soldier killed in Hezbollah drone attack on military base Press TV 11 May 2026 -- A Hezbollah drone strike on a military base in the northern Israeli-occupied territories has killed one Israeli soldier, amid raising fears among Israeli authorities over the resistance movement's warfare capabilities.

  • EU approves new sanctions against Israeli settlers over violence in West Bank Press TV 11 May 2026 -- The European Union has agreed to impose new sanctions against Israeli settlers over the increasing violence against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank.
  • EU ready to approve sanctions against Israeli settlers, says bloc's top diplomat Press TV 11 May 2026 -- High Representative of the European Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy Kaja Kallas says the bloc is ready to approve sanctions against Israeli settlers in the occupied territories.

  • Middle East: Deadly weekend in Lebanon, continued violence in Gaza UN News 11 May 2026 -- The humanitarian situation in Lebanon continues to deteriorate despite the ceasefire announced last month with Israel, the United Nations said on Monday.
  • Emergency Relief Items Airlifted to Lebanon as 1 Million Remain Displaced IOM 11 May 2026 -- More than 4,500 emergency shelter and household items were airlifted from Maastricht to Beirut today through an EU Humanitarian Air Bridge, as humanitarian needs continue to surge across Lebanon. The shipment comes at a critical time, with more than one million people still displaced nationwide, many forced to flee multiple times following repeated displacement orders.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Haiti UNOCHA 11 May 2026 -- Health services targeted and paramedics killed, despite ceasefire

  • China maintains close consultations with ASEAN countries over text of COC: FM spokesperson Global Times 11 May 2026 -- In response to a question on ASEAN's emphasis at last week's ASEAN Summit on the need to conclude a legally binding Code of Conduct in the South China Sea (COC) based on international law, including the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), as well as Philippine President Marcos' remarks at the post-summit press briefing expressing optimism about finalizing the COC within the year, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a regular media briefing on Monday that the formulation of the COC constitutes an important step in implementing the Declaration on the Conduct of Parties in the South China Sea (DOC) and represents an important consensus between China and ASEAN countries.

  • Demarche to Afghan Taliban Regime on Terrorist Attack on Police Post in Bannu Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 11 May 2026 -- The Afghan Chargé d'Affaires was summoned to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs today to deliver a strong demarche regarding the cowardly vehicle-borne IED attack carried out by terrorists of Fitna-al-Khawarij on the Fateh Khel Police Post in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa's Bannu District on 9 May 2026. The attack resulted in the martyrdom of 15 police constables and injuries to four others, including a civilian.

  • ICC Office of the Prosecutor welcomes unsealing of arrest warrant for Ronald Dela Rosa in the Situation in the Philippines ICC 11 May 2026 -- The Office of the Prosecutor of the International Criminal Court welcomes the decision of Pre-Trial Chamber I to unseal the warrant of arrest against Mr Ronald Marapon Dela Rosa, a suspect in the Situation in the Philippines.
  • Situation in the Philippines: ICC judges unseal arrest warrant against Ronald Marapon Dela Rosa for alleged crime against humanity ICC 11 May 2026 -- On 11 May 2026, Pre-Trial Chamber I of the International Criminal Court ("ICC" or "the Court") unsealed an arrest warrant for Ronald Marapon Dela Rosa, which had originally been issued as secret on 6 November 2025.
  • ICC confirms warrant of arrest vs. Dela Rosa Philippine News Agency 11 May 2026 -- The International Criminal Court (ICC) confirmed on Monday night that the warrant of arrest against Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa, which circulated in the media, "is indeed a formal ICC document."

  • Armed drones leading cause of civilian death in Sudan war: UN rights chief UN News 11 May 2026 -- Drones caused more than 80 per cent of civilian deaths in Sudan's war during the first four months of 2026, killing at least 880 people, the UN human rights chief said on Monday, warning that escalating drone warfare could push the conflict into an even deadlier phase.
  • Türk issues high alert on widening Sudan conflict amid increased use of drones OHCHR 11 May 2026 -- UN Human Rights Chief Volker Türk today strongly condemned the rising use of armed unmanned aerial vehicles in Sudan and the deadly impact it is having on civilians, warning of intensified and widening violence in coming weeks that could lead to more displacement and disruption to critical aid flows.

  • Libya - Meeting between Jean-Noël Barrot, minister for europe and foreign Affairs, and ms Hanna Tetteh, special representative of the United nations secretary-general for Libya France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 11 May 2026

  • Chad - Terrorist attacks on May 4 and 6 France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 11 May 2026

News Reports

  • Hantavirus outbreak proves deadly, but pandemic unlikely People's Daily 11 May 2026 -- A deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship scheduled to anchor off Spain's Canary Islands on Sunday has killed three people, with passengers to be transferred directly to waiting aircraft under strict isolation. Global health officials and experts have ruled out a pandemic.

  • More than 25 Defendants Charged in Nationwide Tren de Aragua Crackdown Resulting in the Seizure of Over 80 Firearms and Narcotics in Homeland Security Task Force Investigations US Dept. of Justice 11 May 2026 -- The Department of Justice announced charges against more than 25 individuals as part of a nationwide crackdown targeting the foreign terrorist organization (FTO), Tren de Aragua (TdA).

  • House impeaches VP Sara Duterte with 257 votes Philippine News Agency 11 May 2026 -- With 257 affirmative votes, 25 in the negative and 9 abstentions, the House of Representatives on Monday voted to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte, approving the House Committee on Justice report transmitting the Articles of Impeachment to the Senate for trial.

  • UK targets hostile and heinous Russian activity with latest sanctions UK FCDO 11 May 2026 -- The UK is exposing and taking action against Russia's hostile and heinous activity at every level, from its systematic campaign to forcibly deport and militarise Ukrainian children to recent attempts to interfere in upcoming Armenian elections.
  • Two individuals arrested on suspicion of gross violation of sanctions Swedish Security Service 11 May 2026 -- Two individuals have been arrested on suspicion of gross violation of sanctions. The Swedish Security Service has carried out several interventions and house searches, and extensive investigative efforts are still ongoing.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on May 11, 2026 PRC MofA 11 May 2026
  • Chinese, US top leaders to exchange views on bilateral ties, world peace and development: FM spokesperson on Trump's upcoming China visit Global Times 11 May 2026 -- At the invitation of Chinese President Xi Jinping, US President Donald Trump will pay a state visit to China. This will be another face-to-face meeting between the two heads of state following their meeting in Busan last October, and the first visit to China by a US president in nine years. President Xi will have an in-depth exchange of views with President Trump on major issues concerning China-US relations as well as world peace and development, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday.
  • China announces Trump will pay state visit to China; heads-of-state diplomacy to play irreplaceable role in further stabilizing ties: experts Global Times 11 May 2026 -- China announced on Monday that, at the invitation of Chinese President Xi Jinping, US President Donald Trump will pay a state visit to China from Wednesday to Friday, setting the stage for a closely watched leaders' meeting between the world's two largest economies.
  • Xi, Trump to discuss bilateral relations, world peace and development People's Daily 11 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping and U.S. President Donald Trump will have an in-depth exchange of views on major issues concerning China-U.S. relations and world peace and development, Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday.
  • ICYMI: Ranking Member Shaheen, Senator Curtis Pen Economist Op-Ed Defending U.S. Commitments in the Indo-Pacific Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 11 May 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and John Curtis (R-UT) published an op-ed in The Economist warning that rising aggression from the People's Republic of China toward Taiwan and across the Indo-Pacific requires clear, consistent and bipartisan American leadership.
  • China rejects illegal US sanctions over Iran, vows to protect firms Press TV 11 May 2026 -- China has firmly rejected the latest sanctions imposed by the United States on three of its companies over allegations of enabling Iranian military operations, vowing to protect its businesses from illegal and unilateral restrictions.
  • China ready to work with Russia for more just and equitable global governance system: FM in response to Putin's remarks Global Times 11 May 2026 -- China and Russia are comprehensive strategic partners of coordination for a new era. Under the strategic guidance of the two heads of state, bilateral relations have maintained a high level of development, injecting strong stability into a turbulent and changing world, said Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun on Monday when asked to comment on Russian President Vladimir Putin's remarks at the Victory Day press conference, in which he described cooperation between states such as China and Russia as "undoubtedly a factor of deterrence and stability" in global affairs.
  • U.S. President Donald Trump to pay state visit to China from May 13 to 15 PRC MND 11 May 2026 -- At the invitation of Chinese President Xi Jinping, U.S. President Donald Trump will pay a state visit to China from May 13 to 15, a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson announced on Monday.
  • Tianzhou-10 successfully docks with China Space Station, delivers spacecraft series' 10th consecutive supply run with innovations Global Times 11 May 2026 -- Early on Monday morning, sunlight spilled over the Wenchang Space Launch Site located in the South China's tropical island province of Hainan, glinting off the towering combination of the Long March-7 Y11 rocket and the Tianzhou-10 cargo spacecraft atop the launcher.
  • PhilSA: Chinese rocket debris possibly fell within PH EEZ Philippine News Agency 11 May 2026 -- The Philippine Space Agency (PhilSA) said Monday that debris from China's Long March 7 rocket possibly fell within the Philippine Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ).
  • China's space freighter Tianzhou-10 embarks on spacecraft series' 10th voyage with successful launch Global Times 11 May 2026 -- Early on the Monday morning, sunlight spilled over the Wenchang Space Launch Site located in the South China's tropical island province of Hainan, glinting off the towering combination of the Long March-7 Y11 rocket and the Tianzhou-10 cargo spacecraft atop the launcher.

  • Ex-soldier, Army officer indicted for alleged military leak to China CNA 12 May 2026 -- A former soldier and an active-duty Army officer were indicted Tuesday for allegedly leaking classified military training materials to a Chinese intelligence operative in exchange for monetary benefits.
  • Gov't confident in Taiwan-U.S. ties, cautiously watching Trump-Xi meet: FM CNA 11 May 2026 -- Taiwan's government remains confident in stable relations with the United States but is closely watching how the Taiwan issue will be addressed at a summit between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping (習近平) later this week, Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung (林佳龍) said Monday.
  • Taiwan region cannot participate in WHA due to DPP political manipulation, proving intl community's adherence to one-China principle: Taiwan Affairs Office spokesperson Global Times 11 May 2026 -- When asked to comment on that the registration for 79th World Health Assembly (WHA) has concluded on May 10 and the Taiwan region again cannot participate in it, Chen Binhua, a spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said that this situation is entirely created by the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) authorities, resulting from their political manipulation over the participation of the Taiwan region in this year's WHA and marks the failure of their attempt to seek "Taiwan independence" under the pretext of health affairs. It once again proves that the international community's adherence to the one-China principle is unshakable.
  • China decides not to agree to Taiwan region's participation in this year's WHA: FM Global Times 11 May 2026 -- On the issue of the participation of China's Taiwan region in international organizations, including the activities of the World Health Organization, China's position is consistent and clear: It must be handled in accordance with the one-China principle, a fundamental principle affirmed by Resolution 2758 of the UN General Assembly and Resolution 25.1 of the World Health Assembly (WHA). Without the consent of the central government, China's Taiwan region has no basis, reason or right to participate in the WHA, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday.
  • Ahead of Beijing Summit, Shaheen, Tillis, Bipartisan Colleagues Urge President Trump to Permit Taiwan Arms Sales US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 11 May 2026 -- Last week, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Thom Tillis (R-NC), Chris Coons (D-DE), John Curtis (R-UT), Tammy Duckworth (D-IL), Jacky Rosen (D-NV), Andy Kim (D-NJ) and Elissa Slotkin (D-MI) sent a letter urging President Trump to formally notify $14 billion in U.S. arms sales to Taiwan ahead of his summit with Chinese President Xi later this week.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 11 May 2026

  • President Lee, Canada discuss Middle East, energy supply & demand KOREA.net 11 May 2026 -- President Lee Jae Myung in phone talks with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney has discussed the situation in the Middle East and energy supply and demand.

  • Iran Says Water Shortages Affecting 35 Million People RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- A spokesman for Iran's water industry says that 35 million people in the country are facing water shortages.
  • Iran Executes Man Accused Of Spying For US and Israel RFE/RL 11 May 2026 -- An Iranian man has been executed on allegations of spying for the United States and Israel, reported Mizan, the judiciary's official news outlet.
  • Man convicted of spying for CIA, Mossad executed in Iran Press TV 11 May 2026 -- The death sentence of a man convicted of spying for the CIA and Mossad has been carried out after the verdict was upheld by Iran's Judiciary and all legal procedures were completed.

  • Modi warns Iran war poses great risks to India, urges reductions in fuel use Press TV 11 May 2026 -- Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi warns that the US-Israeli aggression against Iran poses great risks for India, urging citizens to curb fuel use, reduce overseas travel, and pause gold purchases.

  • Kenya - Conversation between Jean-Noël Barrot and his Kenyan counterpart, Musalia Mudavadi France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 11 May 2026
  • Switzerland - m. Jean Noël Barrot's meeting with mr Ignazio Cassis France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 11 May 2026
  • The Daily Press Briefing (05/11/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 11 May 2026

  • At the informal General Affairs Council, the Parliamentary Secretary expressed Latvia's support for opening the EU enlargement negotiation clusters with Ukraine and Moldova as soon as possible Latvia MFA 11 May 2026 -- On 10-11 May 2026, in Nicosia, Cyprus, Artjoms Uršuļskis, the Parliamentary Secretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, participated in an informal meeting of the General Affairs Council of the European Union (EU).

  • Gitanas Nausėda met with the Secretary General of the Council of Europe President of the Republic of Lithuania 11 May 2026 -- President Gitanas Nausėda met on Monday with Alain Berset, Secretary General of the Council of Europe, to discuss security situation in Europe, support for Ukraine, accountability for Russia's crimes of aggression, migration challenges, strengthening democratic resilience, and support for Belarusian civil society and democratic forces.

  • World News in Brief: Human rights in Mongolia, surge in sexual violence in Haiti, worsening hunger in Afghanistan UN News 11 May 2026 -- UN human rights chief Volker Türk on Monday praised Mongolia's recent human rights progress during a visit to the country, which recently adopted the region's first law protecting human rights defenders.
